task_completion.description: Verify that the OpenBCI GUI is open and a Synthetic session is selected or running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI window is visible. The data source is set to 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)' in the control panel, or the session has been started with synthetic data.

task_completion.description: Verify that the data stream is actively running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Data Stream' button has changed to 'Stop Data Stream' (or similar active state indicator), and main graphs show active data waveforms.

task_completion.description: Verify that the EMG widget has been added to the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget panel with the title 'EMG' is visible within the OpenBCI GUI dashboard.

task_completion.description: Verify that the EMG widget's uV Limit is configured to 200.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the EMG widget's interface or settings panel, the 'uV Limit' dropdown or indicator is explicitly set to '200'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the EMG widget is displaying signal activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The EMG widget shows active signal visualizations (e.g., filled bars, moving graphs, or changing numerical values), indicating it is receiving and rendering the synthetic data.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the authentic OpenBCI GUI interface (Processing-based UI with its characteristic dark theme, fonts, and layout), not a text editor or web mockup.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not bypass the GUI by just printing success messages in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal/command prompt is not the primary focus, and there are no printed messages like 'EMG widget added' used as a substitute for actual GUI state.

integrity.description: Ensure the signal activity and widget settings are genuinely rendered by the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The '200' uV limit and EMG signal bars appear naturally integrated into the UI without signs of image manipulation or artificially overlaid text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target widget is 'EMG'. The target uV Limit is '200', which is a valid option in the EmgUVLimit dropdown. The required data mode is 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)'. The VLM should verify that the OpenBCI GUI is running in this mode, the EMG widget is visible on the dashboard, its uV Limit is explicitly set to 200, and it displays active signal data.
